name: InceptionV3
fullName: Rethinking the Inception Architecture for Computer Vision
description: InceptionV3 is a multi-branch convolutional neural network utilizing factorized and parallel convolution blocks to process visual features at multiple spatial receptive fields simultaneously.

educationalNote.summary: Sensory input receiving normalized RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNote.detailed: Accepts normalized images of size 299x299. Larger dimensions preserve features across parallel Inception branches.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial sizes for the multi-branch network.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Larger inputs preserve high resolution visual details.

educationalNote.whyItMatters: Reduces dimensions before the multi-branch stages.

educationalNote.detailed: Retains spatial details while pooling representations.

educationalNote.summary: Concatenates features from all parallel Inception branches.
educationalNote.detailed: Combines 1x1, 3x3, and 5x5 spatial features along the channel axis.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Aggregates multi-scale visual details in a single representation tensor.

educationalNote.detailed: Averages visual representations to a single vector.

groups.name: Input Stem
groups.description: Stem convolutions downsampling input image prior to multi-branch Inception blocks.

groups.name: Inception Module A
groups.description: Applies parallel multi-scale convolutions (1x1, 3x3, 5x5) and combines features.

groups.name: Global Pooling & Classification
groups.description: GAP followed by the dense classifier.
